title: Connect an MCP Server to Your Agent
lede: Bundle your automation flows and tools into an MCP server, then attach it to a Smart Agent so it can call them live during a conversation.

steps.title: Open Automation
steps.content:
1. In the left navigation, go to Intelligence Center → Automation.
  2. This is where you build Flows (multi-step operations) and MCP Servers that expose them to agents.

images.caption: MCP servers built in Automation attach back to your agent in the editor.
MCP servers built in Automation attach back to your agent in the editor.

steps.title: Build and Publish a Flow
steps.content:
1. Create a new flow and chain actions - for example: take a city as input, call a weather API over HTTP, extract the temperature, and return it.
  2. Publish the flow so it can be used inside an MCP server.

steps.title: Create an MCP Server
steps.content:
1. In Automation, open MCP Servers and create a new MCP.
  2. Give it a clear name.
  3. Add tools to it - a predefined Piece or any published Flow (such as your Check Weather flow).

steps.title: Attach the MCP to Your Agent
steps.content:
1. Go to Intelligence Center → Smart Agents and open your agent.
  2. Open Tools / MCP and switch to MCP Servers.
  3. Attach the MCP you created. You can also click Add custom MCP server to connect an external MCP by URL (an /sse or /mcp endpoint) with an optional auth header.

images.alt: MCP servers in the Tools panel
images.caption: Attach an MCP server under Tools / MCP → MCP Servers.

images.alt: MCP servers in the Tools panel
images.caption: Attach an MCP server under Tools / MCP → MCP Servers.

steps.title: Test the Integration
steps.content:
1. Open Test Agent and connect.
  2. Ask something that should trigger the MCP tool (for example "What's the weather in Paris today?").
  3. Confirm the agent calls the flow and returns the right result. You can review each invocation and its inputs/outputs in the MCP's history in Automation.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?
nextSteps.description: Keep building on your agent's capabilities:

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io

  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
